prompt: Implement and export a TypeScript function `longestIncreasing(nums: number[]): number[]` returning a longest STRICTLY increasing subsequence of `nums` (elements need not be contiguous; relative order preserved). The returned array must (a) be strictly increasing, (b) be a subsequence of the input, and (c) have the maximum possible such length. If several exist, any one of maximum length is accepted. An empty input returns []; when all elements are equal or strictly decreasing, the answer has length 1. Use a named export. Output ONLY the complete file as a single fenced TypeScript code block.
check: bun -e 'const m=await import(process.env.OB1_FILE);const f=m.longestIncreasing??m.default;if(typeof f!=="function")process.exit(2);const isSub=(sub,arr)=>{let i=0;for(const x of arr){if(i<sub.length&&x===sub[i])i++;}return i===sub.length;};const strictInc=(s)=>s.every((x,i)=>i===0||s[i-1]<x);const lisLen=(a)=>{if(!a.length)return 0;const dp=a.map(()=>1);for(let i=0;i<a.length;i++)for(let j=0;j<i;j++)if(a[j]<a[i]&&dp[j]+1>dp[i])dp[i]=dp[j]+1;return Math.max(...dp);};const cases=[[10,9,2,5,3,7,101,18],[0,1,0,3,2,3],[7,7,7,7],[],[5,4,3,2,1],[1,2,3,4,5],[3,1,2,1,2,3]];for(const c of cases){const r=f(c.slice());if(!Array.isArray(r)){console.error("not array");process.exit(1);}if(!isSub(r,c)){console.error("not subseq "+JSON.stringify(r)+" of "+JSON.stringify(c));process.exit(1);}if(!strictInc(r)){console.error("not strictly increasing "+JSON.stringify(r));process.exit(1);}if(r.length!==lisLen(c)){console.error("len "+r.length+" != "+lisLen(c)+" for "+JSON.stringify(c));process.exit(1);}}'

prompt: Implement and export a TypeScript function `editScript(a: string, b: string)` returning an object { distance: number, ops: Op[] } describing a MINIMUM-cost edit sequence transforming `a` into `b` under Levenshtein rules (insert, delete, substitute each cost 1; keeping an equal character costs 0). `distance` is that minimum. `ops` is applied left-to-right to reconstruct `b`, each element exactly one of: { op: "keep", char } (next char of a, equal to char, copied); { op: "delete", char } (next char of a, equal to char, removed); { op: "insert", char } (char appended); { op: "substitute", from, to } (next char of a, equal to from, replaced by to). Every keep/delete/substitute consumes one char of a in order; insert consumes none; after all ops the whole input a must be consumed and the output must equal b, and the count of non-keep ops must equal distance. Any minimum-distance script is accepted. Use a named export. Output ONLY the complete file as a single fenced TypeScript code block.
check: bun -e 'const m=await import(process.env.OB1_FILE);const f=m.editScript??m.default;if(typeof f!=="function")process.exit(2);const lev=(a,b)=>{const dp=[];for(let i=0;i<=a.length;i++){dp.push([]);for(let j=0;j<=b.length;j++)dp[i][j]=i===0?j:j===0?i:0;}for(let i=1;i<=a.length;i++)for(let j=1;j<=b.length;j++)dp[i][j]=a[i-1]===b[j-1]?dp[i-1][j-1]:1+Math.min(dp[i-1][j],dp[i][j-1],dp[i-1][j-1]);return dp[a.length][b.length];};const apply=(a,ops)=>{let i=0,out="";for(const o of ops){if(o.op==="keep"){if(a[i]!==o.char)return null;out+=a[i];i++;}else if(o.op==="delete"){if(a[i]!==o.char)return null;i++;}else if(o.op==="insert"){out+=o.char;}else if(o.op==="substitute"){if(a[i]!==o.from)return null;out+=o.to;i++;}else return null;}return i===a.length?out:null;};const cases=[["kitten","sitting"],["","abc"],["abc",""],["abc","abc"],["flaw","lawn"],["a","b"],["intention","execution"],["ab","ba"]];for(const [a,b] of cases){const r=f(a,b);const d=lev(a,b);if(!r||typeof r.distance!=="number"){console.error("bad result");process.exit(1);}if(r.distance!==d){console.error("dist "+r.distance+" != "+d+" for "+a+"/"+b);process.exit(1);}const out=apply(a,r.ops);if(out!==b){console.error("apply "+JSON.stringify(out)+" != "+b);process.exit(1);}const nk=r.ops.filter(o=>o.op!=="keep").length;if(nk!==d){console.error("nonkeep "+nk+" != "+d+" for "+a+"/"+b);process.exit(1);}}'

prompt: Implement and export a TypeScript function `jsonPointerGet(doc: unknown, pointer: string): unknown` implementing RFC 6901 evaluation. The `pointer` is either the empty string (referring to the WHOLE document) or a string of reference tokens each prefixed by '/'. Within a token the escape sequences decode as ~1 -> '/' and ~0 -> '~' (decode ~1 BEFORE ~0). Each decoded token selects an object member by that exact key, or, when the current value is an array, an element by decimal index (0-based; the token must be a valid non-negative integer with no leading zeros other than "0" itself). If a token references a missing object key, an out-of-range or malformed array index, or descends into a primitive, THROW an Error. Assume the document is composed of plain JSON values. Use a named export. Output ONLY the complete file as a single fenced TypeScript code block.
check: bun -e 'const m=await import(process.env.OB1_FILE);const f=m.jsonPointerGet??m.default;if(typeof f!=="function")process.exit(2);const J=JSON.stringify;const doc={foo:["bar","baz"],"":0,"a/b":1,"m~n":8," ":7,"~1":9,nested:{x:[10,{y:20}]}};const eq=(p,v)=>{const r=f(doc,p);if(J(r)!==J(v)){console.error(J(p)+" => "+J(r)+" != "+J(v));process.exit(1);}};if(f(doc,"")!==doc){console.error("whole doc identity");process.exit(1);}eq("/foo",["bar","baz"]);eq("/foo/0","bar");eq("/foo/1","baz");eq("/",0);eq("/a~1b",1);eq("/m~0n",8);eq("/ ",7);eq("/~01",9);eq("/nested/x/0",10);eq("/nested/x/1/y",20);const mustThrow=(p)=>{let t=false;try{f(doc,p);}catch(e){t=true;}if(!t){console.error("expected throw: "+J(p));process.exit(1);}};mustThrow("/foo/2");mustThrow("/foo/x");mustThrow("/nope");mustThrow("/foo/0/z");mustThrow("/foo/01");'

prompt: Implement and export a TypeScript function `addBusinessDays(date: string, n: number): string`. `date` is a calendar date in YYYY-MM-DD format interpreted in UTC. Return the date `n` business days away, formatted YYYY-MM-DD. Business days are Monday-Friday; Saturday and Sunday are skipped. Move forward when n > 0 and backward when n < 0: step one calendar day at a time in that direction and count a step only when it lands on a weekday, until |n| business days have been counted (so a Friday + 1 business day is the following Monday). When n === 0 return the input date unchanged, even if it falls on a weekend. All arithmetic must use UTC. Use a named export. Output ONLY the complete file as a single fenced TypeScript code block.
check: bun -e 'const m=await import(process.env.OB1_FILE);const f=m.addBusinessDays??m.default;if(typeof f!=="function")process.exit(2);const ref=(date,n)=>{const [Y,M,D]=date.split("-").map(Number);let t=Date.UTC(Y,M-1,D);const step=n>0?1:-1;let cnt=Math.abs(n);while(cnt>0){t+=step*86400000;const d=new Date(t).getUTCDay();if(d!==0&&d!==6)cnt--;}const dt=new Date(t);const p=(x)=>String(x).padStart(2,"0");return dt.getUTCFullYear()+"-"+p(dt.getUTCMonth()+1)+"-"+p(dt.getUTCDate());};const eq=(date,n)=>{const got=f(date,n),want=ref(date,n);if(got!==want){console.error(date+" +"+n+" => "+got+" != "+want);process.exit(1);}};const cases=[["2026-07-06",1],["2026-07-06",5],["2026-07-06",6],["2026-07-10",1],["2026-07-06",-1],["2026-07-31",1],["2025-12-31",2],["2024-02-28",1],["2026-01-02",-1],["2026-07-04",-3],["2026-07-05",1]];for(const [d,n] of cases)eq(d,n);const same=(d)=>{if(f(d,0)!==d){console.error("n=0 must be unchanged: "+d+" => "+f(d,0));process.exit(1);}};same("2026-07-04");same("2026-07-05");same("2026-07-06");'

prompt: Implement and export a TypeScript function `repairParens(s: string): string`. The input consists only of '(' and ')'. Insert the MINIMUM number of parentheses so the result is balanced. To make the result unique, use exactly this procedure: scan left to right tracking the count of currently-unmatched '('. On '(' increment the counter and append '(' to the output. On ')', if the counter is > 0 decrement it and append ')'; otherwise (an unmatched ')') append '(' and then ')' to the output (so the output gains "()"), leaving the counter at 0. After the scan append one ')' for each still-unmatched '(' (the counter's value). Return the repaired string. An empty input returns the empty string and an already-balanced input is returned unchanged. Use a named export. Output ONLY the complete file as a single fenced TypeScript code block.
check: bun -e 'const m=await import(process.env.OB1_FILE);const f=m.repairParens??m.default;if(typeof f!=="function")process.exit(2);const ref=(s)=>{let out="",open=0;for(const c of s){if(c==="("){open++;out+="(";}else{if(open>0){open--;out+=")";}else{out+="()";}}}return out+")".repeat(open);};const minins=(s)=>{let open=0,ins=0;for(const c of s){if(c==="(")open++;else{if(open>0)open--;else ins++;}}return ins+open;};const cases=["","()","(",")","())","(((",")(","()())(","(()","))((","()()"];for(const x of cases){const r=f(x);if(r!==ref(x)){console.error(JSON.stringify(x)+" => "+JSON.stringify(r)+" != "+JSON.stringify(ref(x)));process.exit(1);}if(r.length-x.length!==minins(x)){console.error("insertion count wrong for "+JSON.stringify(x));process.exit(1);}}'

prompt: Implement and export a TypeScript function `expand(input: string): string` that decodes a string in this grammar (assume well-formed input). The input is DICT then '|' then BODY (the '|' separator appears exactly once). DICT is zero or more entries separated by ';'; each entry is KEY=SEQ where KEY is a single uppercase letter A-Z and SEQ is a sequence; empty DICT (nothing before '|') means no entries. A SEQUENCE is a concatenation of zero or more UNITS. A UNIT is one of: a lowercase letter a-z (a literal expanding to itself); <count>(<sequence>) where <count> is one or more decimal digits, expanding to the inner sequence's expansion repeated <count> times (a count of 0 yields the empty string; repeats may nest); or an uppercase letter A-Z, a reference expanding to that key's expansion, which is always DEFINED EARLIER in DICT (no forward or self references). BODY is a SEQUENCE and may reference any key in DICT. Return the fully expanded BODY. Use a named export. Output ONLY the complete file as a single fenced TypeScript code block.

Implement and export a TypeScript function `maxSchedule(intervals: number[][]): number[][]` selecting the LARGEST possible set of pairwise non-overlapping intervals (activity selection). Each interval is [start, end] with start <= end; two intervals overlap iff a.start < b.end && b.start < a.end (so intervals that only touch at an endpoint, like [1,2] and [2,3], do NOT overlap and may both be chosen). To make the result deterministic use exactly this greedy: sort intervals by end ascending, breaking ties by start ascending; scan them in that order and select an interval whenever its start is >= the end of the most recently selected interval (the first is always selected). Return the selected intervals in selection order (i.e. by increasing end). Do not mutate the input. An empty input returns []. Use a named export. Output ONLY the complete file as a single fenced TypeScript code block.
check: bun -e 'const m=await import(process.env.OB1_FILE);const f=m.maxSchedule??m.default;if(typeof f!=="function")process.exit(2);const J=JSON.stringify;const ref=(iv)=>{const s=iv.map(x=>x.slice()).sort((a,b)=>a[1]-b[1]||a[0]-b[0]);const out=[];let end=-Infinity;for(const it of s){if(it[0]>=end){out.push(it);end=it[1];}}return out;};const overlaps=(a,b)=>a[0]<b[1]&&b[0]<a[1];const brute=(iv)=>{let best=0;const n=iv.length;for(let mask=0;mask<(1<<n);mask++){const sel=[];for(let i=0;i<n;i++)if(mask&(1<<i))sel.push(iv[i]);let ok=true;for(let i=0;i<sel.length&&ok;i++)for(let j=i+1;j<sel.length;j++)if(overlaps(sel[i],sel[j])){ok=false;break;}if(ok)best=Math.max(best,sel.length);}return best;};const cases=[[],[[1,2]],[[1,3],[2,4],[3,5]],[[1,2],[2,3],[3,4]],[[0,6],[1,2],[2,3],[3,4]],[[1,4],[2,3],[3,5],[0,6],[5,7]]];for(const c of cases){const r=f(c.map(x=>x.slice()));if(J(r)!==J(ref(c))){console.error(J(c)+" => "+J(r)+" != "+J(ref(c)));process.exit(1);}for(let i=0;i<r.length;i++)for(let j=i+1;j<r.length;j++)if(overlaps(r[i],r[j])){console.error("overlap in result");process.exit(1);}if(r.length!==brute(c)){console.error("not maximum "+J(c)+" got "+r.length+" want "+brute(c));process.exit(1);}}'

prompt: Implement and export a TypeScript function `wordBreak(s: string, dict: string[]): string[] | null`. Determine whether `s` can be segmented into a sequence of one or more words, each appearing in `dict` (words may be reused any number of times). If it can, return one such segmentation as an array of the words in order (their concatenation must equal s); if it cannot, return null. The empty string returns [] (the empty segmentation). Assume dict contains only non-empty strings. Use a named export. Output ONLY the complete file as a single fenced TypeScript code block.
check: bun -e 'const m=await import(process.env.OB1_FILE);const f=m.wordBreak??m.default;if(typeof f!=="function")process.exit(2);const canSeg=(s,dict)=>{const set=new Set(dict);const dp=Array(s.length+1).fill(false);dp[0]=true;for(let i=1;i<=s.length;i++)for(let j=0;j<i;j++)if(dp[j]&&set.has(s.slice(j,i))){dp[i]=true;break;}return dp[s.length];};const cases=[["leetcode",["leet","code"]],["applepenapple",["apple","pen"]],["catsandog",["cats","dog","sand","and","cat"]],["",["a"]],["aaaaaaa",["aaaa","aaa"]],["ab",["a"]],["a",["a"]],["aaa",["aa","aaa"]]];for(const [s,dict] of cases){const r=f(s,dict.slice());const seg=canSeg(s,dict);if(seg){if(!Array.isArray(r)){console.error("expected array for "+s+" got "+JSON.stringify(r));process.exit(1);}if(r.join("")!==s){console.error("concat "+JSON.stringify(r)+" != "+s);process.exit(1);}const set=new Set(dict);for(const w of r)if(!set.has(w)){console.error("word not in dict: "+w);process.exit(1);}}else{if(r!==null){console.error("expected null for "+s+" got "+JSON.stringify(r));process.exit(1);}}}'
